name: dialog
description: An accessible modal dialog. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): open state lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the body + footer are owned template regions, not slots. Replaces the <lv-dialog> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.
docs: Register DialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it. Put your dialog body + footer markup directly in the data-lv-dialog-body / data-lv-dialog-footer regions of the copied template (they are owned source, NOT slots). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button, so Escape and the button take the same wire path), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).
